actions.name: APP
actions.description: Unified app control. action=launch starts a registered app; action=relaunch stops then launches (optionally with verify); action=list shows installed + running apps; action=load_from_directory registers apps from an absolute folder; action=create runs the multi-turn create-or-edit flow that searches existing apps, asks new/edit/cancel, scaffolds from the min-app template, and dispatches a coding agent with AppVerificationService validator.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation: launch | relaunch | load_from_directory | list | create.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: app
parameters.description: App name, slug, or display name (launch / relaunch / create-edit).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Alias for `app`.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: Specific run id to stop before relaunching (relaunch mode).

parameters.name: verify
parameters.description: When true, runs AppVerificationService (fast profile) after relaunch.

parameters.name: workdir
parameters.description: Absolute workdir for verify (relaunch) or for explicit edit (create).

parameters.name: directory
parameters.description: Absolute directory to scan (load_from_directory mode).

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Free-form description of the app to build (create mode). Defaults to the user message text.

parameters.name: editTarget
parameters.description: Skip the picker and edit this installed app directly (create mode).

parameters.name: choice
parameters.description: Override choice reply (`new` | `edit-N` | `cancel`) for create mode follow-up turns.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Manage apps: launch/relaunch/list/load folder/create; create scaffolds min app, runs coding agent, verifies result.

exampleCalls.user: Use APP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BLOCK
actions.description: Block or unblock phone apps and desktop websites.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Which surface to act on: 'app' (phone apps) or 'website' (desktop hosts-file/SelfControl).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: One of: block, unblock, status, request_permission, release, list_active.

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Free-form description of what the owner wants. Used by the block action to extract apps/hostnames + duration.

parameters.name: hostnames
parameters.description: (target=website) Public hostnames or URLs to block, e.g. ['x.com','twitter.com'].

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: (target=website) Set true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, block returns a draft confirmation request. Required by release.

parameters.name: ruleId
parameters.description: (target=website, action=release) ID of the managed block rule to release.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: (target=website, action=release) Optional reason recorded on the rule when released.

parameters.name: includeLiveStatus
parameters.description: (target=website, action=list_active) Include the current hosts-file/SelfControl live block state. Default true.

parameters.name: includeManagedRules
parameters.description: (target=website, action=list_active) Include managed owner block rules. Default true.

parameters.name: packageNames
parameters.description: (target=app, Android) Package names to block, e.g. ['com.twitter.android'].

parameters.name: appTokens
parameters.description: (target=app, iOS) iPhone app tokens from a previous selectApps() call.

parameters.name: durationMinutes
parameters.description: How long to block, in minutes. Omit/null for an indefinite block that stays active until manually removed.

actions.descriptionCompressed: block/unblock apps+websites; actions block|unblock|status|request_permission|release|list_active; web requires confirmed:true

exampleCalls.user: Use BLOCK with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BROWSER
actions.description: Single BROWSER action — control whichever browser target is registered. Targets are pluggable: `workspace` (electrobun-embedded BrowserView, the default; falls back to a JSDOM web mode when the desktop bridge isn't configured), `bridge` (the user's real Chrome/Safari via the Agent Browser Bridge companion extension), and `computeruse` (a local puppeteer-driven Chromium via plugin-computeruse). The agent uses what is available — the BrowserService picks the active target when none is specified. Use `action: "autofill_login"` with `domain` (and optional `username`, `submit`) to vault-gated autofill into an open workspace tab.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Browser action to perform. Snake_case values are canonical; legacy kebab-case and subaction are also accepted.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: tabAction
parameters.description: Tab operation when subaction is tab

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Required when action is autofill_login: registrable hostname (e.g. `github.com`).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: When using autofill-login: specific saved login; omit for most recently modified.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: When using autofill-login: submit the form after filling (default false).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Session or tab id to target

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL for open or navigate

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector for click, type, or wait

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text for type

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Keyboard key for press

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: Scroll distance in pixels

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Command timeout in milliseconds

parameters.name: script
parameters.description: Script for eval

parameters.name: watchMode
parameters.description: Hint that the user is watching; prefers realistic-* subactions for click/fill so the cursor moves visibly and pointer events fire faithfully.

parameters.name: cursorDurationMs
parameters.description: Cursor animation duration (ms) for realistic-* subactions

parameters.name: perCharDelayMs
parameters.description: Per-character delay for realistic-type/realistic-fill (ms)

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: Replace existing input value when filling (vs append) — applies to realistic-fill

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Cursor target X (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Cursor target Y (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

actions.descriptionCompressed: Browser tab/page control: open/navigate/click/type/screenshot/state; action autofill_login + domain autofill vault-gated credential into workspace tab pre-authorized in Settings Vault Logins. Bridge settings/status use MANAGE_BROWSER_BRIDGE.

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CALENDAR
actions.description: Manage Google Calendar plus availability and meeting preferences. Subactions:

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Which calendar operation to run. Google Calendar: feed, next_event, search_events, create_event, update_event, delete_event, trip_window, bulk_reschedule. Availability: check_availability, propose_times. Preferences: update_preferences.

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Natural-language description of the calendar request (e.g. "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm").

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Event title when creating a calendar event.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search phrase for search_events / travel_itinerary (e.g. flight, dentist, Denver).

parameters.name: queries
parameters.description: Optional array of search phrases for search_events. Combined and deduped.

parameters.name: details
parameters.description: Structured calendar fields — time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
parameters.descriptionCompressed: calendar details: calendarId timeMin timeMax timeZone startAt endAt durationMinutes eventId newTitle description location travelOriginAddress windowDays windowPreset forceSync

parameters.name: durationMinutes
parameters.description: Meeting length in minutes. Used by propose_times.

parameters.name: daysAhead
parameters.description: Days ahead for propose_times search window (defaults to 7, ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied).

parameters.name: slotCount
parameters.description: Number of candidate slots for propose_times (defaults to 3).

parameters.name: windowStart
parameters.description: ISO-8601 earliest start of the propose_times search window.

parameters.name: windowEnd
parameters.description: ISO-8601 latest end of the propose_times search window.

parameters.name: startAt
parameters.description: ISO-8601 start time. Used by check_availability.

parameters.name: endAt
parameters.description: ISO-8601 end time. Used by check_availability.

parameters.name: timeZone
parameters.description: IANA time zone for update_preferences (interprets preferred hours).

parameters.name: preferredStartLocal
parameters.description: Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

parameters.name: preferredEndLocal
parameters.description: Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

parameters.name: defaultDurationMinutes
parameters.description: Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).

parameters.name: travelBufferMinutes
parameters.description: Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).

parameters.name: blackoutWindows
parameters.description: Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.
parameters.descriptionCompressed: blackoutWindows[]: label startLocal HH:MM endLocal HH:MM daysOfWeek?[0..6]

actions.descriptionCompressed: calendar event CRUD + availability + prefs; subactions create_event|update_event|delete_event|search_events|propose_times|check_availability|next_event|feed

exampleCalls.user: Use CALENDAR with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CALENDLY
actions.description: Work with Calendly specifically (calendly.com / api.calendly.com).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link. When omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Optional free-form description of the user's intent.

parameters.name: eventTypeUri
parameters.description: Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.

actions.descriptionCompressed: calendly: list_event_types|availability|upcoming_events|single_use_link; route here for calendly.com URLs

exampleCalls.user: Use CALENDLY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: COMPUTER_USE
actions.description:
computer_use:
  purpose: Canonical cross-platform computer-use action for real desktop interaction on macOS, Linux, and Windows when direct computer operation is required.
  guidance: Take a screenshot before acting. After each desktop action, the result includes a screenshot when available. Use this standard plugin action, not a LifeOps wrapper, for Finder/Desktop/native-app/browser/file/terminal workflows on the owner's machine.
  actions: screenshot/click/click_with_modifiers/double_click/right_click/mouse_move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Desktop action to perform.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Target [x, y] pixel coordinate.

parameters.name: startCoordinate
parameters.description: Start [x, y] pixel coordinate for drag.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description: Modifier keys to hold during click_with_modifiers, e.g. ['cmd', 'shift'] or ['ctrl'].

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Single key or combo string depending on action.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: clicks
parameters.description: Number of clicks for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: scrollDirection
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: scrollAmount
parameters.description: Scroll tick count.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Canonical cross-platform desktop control: screenshot/click/modified click/double/right/move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

exampleCalls.user: Use COMPUTER_USE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CONNECTOR
actions.description: Manage **account** state for installed connectors: connect (log in),

parameters.name: connector
parameters.description: Which connector to manage (kind from ConnectorRegistry, e.g. google, x, telegram, signal, discord, imessage, whatsapp, twilio, calendly, duffel, health, browser_bridge). Optional when subaction=list.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Lifecycle operation. connect (start auth/pairing); disconnect (revoke + clear grant); verify (active read/send probe where available). status/list are read-only diagnostics for explicit troubleshooting; prefer provider/core registry context when available. Strongly preferred - when omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: owner | agent. Defaults to owner.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Connection mode: local | cloud_managed | remote. Defaults vary by connector.

parameters.name: recentLimit
parameters.description: verify only — how many recent messages/dialogs to read where the connector supports passive reads.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Discord verify only — optional search text to prove browser-message reads.

parameters.name: sendTarget
parameters.description: verify only — destination chat/recipient/channel for the self-test send.

parameters.name: sendMessage
parameters.description: verify only — text body for the self-test send.

parameters.name: browser
parameters.description: browser_bridge connect only — chrome | safari.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: browser_bridge connect only — profile identifier within the browser.

parameters.name: profileLabel
parameters.description: browser_bridge connect only — human-readable profile label.

parameters.name: redirectUrl
parameters.description: google/x connect only — optional OAuth redirect URL override.

actions.descriptionCompressed: account-level connector lifecycle: connect(log in)|disconnect(log out)|verify|status|list; registry-driven kinds; for plugin install/uninstall use PLUGIN

exampleCalls.user: Use CONNECTOR with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CREDENTIALS
actions.description: Owner-only password and autofill operations across browser autofill (LifeOps extension) and the OS password manager (1Password / ProtonPass).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: fill | whitelist_add | whitelist_list (autofill) | search | list | inject_username | inject_password (password manager).

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: (action=fill) One of email, password, name, phone, custom. Tells the password manager which field to resolve.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: (action=fill | whitelist_add) Domain to act on. For fill, used as the tab URL when url is omitted.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: (action=fill) Optional explicit tab URL (used for whitelist enforcement).

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: (action=search) Natural-language description of the lookup intent.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: (action=search) Search string matched against item title, URL, username, and tags.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: (action=inject_username | inject_password) Password manager item id.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: (action=list) Optional item limit (default 20).

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Required true for whitelist_add and for either inject_* action. Ensures the owner approved the change.

actions.descriptionCompressed: credentials: fill|whitelist_add|whitelist_list|search|list|inject_username|inject_password; clipboard-only; confirmed:true required for inject and whitelist_add

exampleCalls.user: Use CREDENTIALS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: ENTITY
actions.description: Manage people, organizations, projects, and concepts the owner cares about, plus typed relationships between them. Subactions: add, list, set_identity, set_relationship, log_interaction, merge. Use SCHEDULED_TASK for follow-up cadence; use LIFE for one-off dated reminders to call/text someone.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Which ENTITY operation to run: add (new contact), list (read rolodex), log_interaction (record contact event), set_identity (force-merge a platform handle onto an entity), set_relationship (typed edge between entities), merge (collapse duplicate entities). Follow-up cadence belongs to SCHEDULED_TASKS.
parameters.descriptionCompressed: ENTITY op: add | list | log_interaction | set_identity | set_relationship | merge

parameters.name: intent
parameters.description: Free-form user intent used to infer action when not set.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Contact display name. When relationshipId is omitted, the handler resolves an existing contact by this name.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Primary channel for the contact (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp).

parameters.name: handle
parameters.description: Primary handle/address on the chosen channel.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Optional email address for the contact.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Optional phone number for the contact.

parameters.name: notes
parameters.description: Free-form notes or interaction summary.

parameters.name: relationshipId
parameters.description: Target relationship id.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional reason note.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Optional explicit confirmation flag.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: Target entity id. Used by set_identity (force a new identity onto a known entity), merge (target id), and any operation that needs a stable EntityStore id.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Identity platform for set_identity (e.g. telegram, slack, email, twitter). Combine with handle.

parameters.name: displayName
parameters.description: Display name shown alongside an observed identity (set_identity).

parameters.name: toEntityId
parameters.description: Target entity id for set_relationship.

parameters.name: fromEntityId
parameters.description: Source entity id for set_relationship. Defaults to 'self' when omitted.

parameters.name: relationshipType
parameters.description: Edge type label for set_relationship (e.g. manages, colleague_of, works_at, partner_of, family_of).
parameters.descriptionCompressed: set_relationship edge type label e.g. manages|colleague_of|works_at|partner_of

parameters.name: sourceEntityIds
parameters.description: Entity ids being folded into the target entity (merge). Provide as a JSON array of strings.

parameters.name: evidence
parameters.description: Free-form evidence string captured alongside set_identity / set_relationship observations.

actions.descriptionCompressed: people+relationships: add|list|set_identity|set_relationship|log_interaction|merge; follow-up cadence → SCHEDULED_TASK

exampleCalls.user: Use ENTITY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: FILE
actions.description: Read, write, edit, search, find, or list workspace files through one FILE action. Choose action=read/write/edit/grep/glob/ls. All paths must be absolute unless an operation explicitly defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: File operation to run.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path for read/write/edit operations.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute file or directory path for grep/glob/ls. Defaults to the session cwd where supported.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Full file contents for action=write.

parameters.name: old_string
parameters.description: Exact substring to replace for action=edit.

parameters.name: new_string
parameters.description: Replacement substring for action=edit.

parameters.name: replace_all
parameters.description: For action=edit, replace every occurrence instead of requiring one match.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Regex for action=grep or glob pattern for action=glob.

parameters.name: glob
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep glob filter for action=grep.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep file type for action=grep.

parameters.name: output_mode
parameters.description: For action=grep: content, files_with_matches, or count.

parameters.name: -A
parameters.description: For action=grep content mode, lines after each match.

parameters.name: -B
parameters.description: For action=grep content mode, lines before each match.

parameters.name: -C
parameters.description: For action=grep content mode, lines around each match.

parameters.name: case_insensitive
parameters.description: For action=grep, match case-insensitively.

parameters.name: multiline
parameters.description: For action=grep, enable multiline regex matching.

parameters.name: head_limit
parameters.description: For action=grep, truncate output to the first N lines.

parameters.name: show_line_numbers
parameters.description: For action=grep, include 1-based line numbers in content output.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: For action=read, zero-based line offset.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: For action=read, max number of lines to return.

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: For action=ls, glob patterns to exclude.

actions.descriptionCompressed: File operations umbrella: action=read/write/edit/grep/glob/ls.

exampleCalls.user: Use FILE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GITHUB
actions.description: GitHub umbrella for pull requests, issues, and notification triage. Use action=pr_list/pr_review/issue_create/issue_assign/issue_close/issue_reopen/issue_comment/issue_label/notification_triage.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: GitHub operation to run.

parameters.name: repo

parameters.name: number

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: PR state for pr_list: open, closed, or all.

parameters.name: author

parameters.name: review_action
parameters.description: For action=pr_review: approve, request-changes, or comment.

parameters.name: title

parameters.name: body

parameters.name: assignees

parameters.name: labels

parameters.name: as

parameters.name: accountId

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true for GitHub write operations.

actions.descriptionCompressed: GitHub: pr_list|pr_review|issue_create|issue_assign|issue_close|issue_reopen|issue_comment|issue_label|notification_triage

actions.name: LINEAR
actions.description: Manage Linear issues, comments, and activity. Operations: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. The op is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.name: MANAGE_BROWSER_BRIDGE
actions.description: Owner-only management of the Agent Browser Bridge companion extension that connects Eliza to the user's Chrome and Safari browsers. Actions: refresh (show settings/status/connection state), install (build and reveal the extension for setup), reveal_folder (open the built extension folder), open_manager (open chrome://extensions only when the owner explicitly asks). The action parameter is inferred from message text when omitted; show/settings/status maps to refresh and 'open chrome extensions' maps to open_manager. Prefer the browser-bridge provider for passive companion status and use this action's refresh child action only for an explicit live refresh.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Bridge management action. Use refresh for show/settings/status/connection-state requests. Use open_manager only for explicit chrome://extensions or extension-manager requests. install builds/reveals/opens setup; reveal_folder opens the build folder. Inferred from message text if omitted.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Manage LifeOps Browser Bridge: refresh shows settings/status; install setup; reveal_folder build folder; open_manager chrome://extensions.

exampleCalls.user: Use MANAGE_BROWSER_BRIDGE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MC

Choose one action: connect (host?,port?,username?,auth?,version?), disconnect, goto (x,y,z), stop, look (yaw,pitch), control (control,state,durationMs?), waypoint_goto (name), dig (x,y,z), place (x,y,z,face), chat (message), attack (entityId), waypoint_set (name), waypoint_delete (name).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to run.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional JSON object containing the fields required by the chosen op.

actions.name: MCP

parameters.name: toolName

parameters.name: arguments

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: For action=read_resource: exact MCP resource URI to read.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Natural-language description of the tool call or resource to select; for action=search_actions, the keyword query.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: For action=search_actions: filter results to a single connected platform.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: For action=search_actions: maximum results to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: For action=search_actions: skip first N results for pagination.

actions.name: MUSIC
actions.description: Unified music action. Use verb-shaped action for everything:

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Verb-shaped subaction. Playback: play, pause, resume, skip, stop.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search/play/queue query depending on subaction.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Direct media URL when using play_audio or play.

parameters.name: playlistName
parameters.description: Playlist name for playlist_play / playlist_save.

parameters.name: song
parameters.description: Song query when adding to a playlist.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Search result limit (search / library helpers).

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true when the underlying operation requires confirmation.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Structured routing operation when using set_routing (set_mode, start_route, …).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Routing mode for set_routing operations.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Stream/source id for set_routing.

actions.name: PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES
actions.description: Propose concrete meeting time slots to offer to another person. This is

parameters.name: durationMinutes

parameters.description: Number of candidate slots to return. Defaults to 3.

parameters.name: windowEnd
parameters.description: Optional ISO-8601 latest end for the search window.

parameters.name: timeZone
parameters.description: Optional IANA time zone override when the user is temporarily traveling and wants proposals shown in that local time.

parameters.name: pairingCode
parameters.description: 6-digit one-time pairing code for start. Required unless ELIZA_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.

parameters.name: requesterIdentity
parameters.description: Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit on start.

parameters.name: reason

Pick one action: walk_to (x,z,run?), attack (npcId), chat_public (message), eat (item?), drop (item), set_goal (title,notes?), complete_goal (status?,goalId?,notes?), remember (notes,kind?,weight?). Returns success and a short status message; the autonomous loop already handles its own dispatch — this is the planner-facing surface.

actions.name: SCHEDULED_TASKS
actions.description: Manage the owner's scheduled-task spine: reminders, check-ins, follow-ups, approvals, recaps, watchers, outputs, and custom tasks. Actions: list, get, create, update, snooze, skip, complete, acknowledge, dismiss, cancel, reopen, history.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Which scheduled-task operation to run: list | get | create | update | snooze | skip | complete | acknowledge | dismiss | cancel | reopen | history.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: Target taskId for get / update / snooze / skip / complete / acknowledge / dismiss / cancel / reopen / history.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: ScheduledTaskKind for create + filter for list. One of reminder, checkin, followup, approval, recap, watcher, output, custom.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status filter for list (string or string[]). One of scheduled, fired, acknowledged, completed, skipped, expired, failed, dismissed.

parameters.name: subjectKind
parameters.description: ScheduledTaskSubject.kind: entity | relationship | thread | document | calendar_event | self.

parameters.name: subjectId
parameters.description: ScheduledTaskSubject.id paired with subjectKind.

parameters.name: ownerVisibleOnly
parameters.description: When true, list returns only ownerVisible tasks.

parameters.name: promptInstructions
parameters.description: create-only: prompt instructions stored on the task.

parameters.name: trigger
parameters.description: create-only: ScheduledTaskTrigger object (once / cron / interval / relative_to_anchor / during_window / event / manual / after_task).

parameters.name: contextRequest
parameters.description: create-only: structured context request for owner facts, entities, relationships, recent task states, or event payload.

parameters.name: shouldFire
parameters.description: create-only: structural gate composition; use gate refs rather than prompt text conditions.

parameters.name: completionCheck
parameters.description: create-only: structural completion check such as user_replied_within, user_acknowledged, subject_updated, or health_signal_observed.

parameters.name: output
parameters.description: create-only: output destination/target, e.g. { destination: 'channel', target: 'in_app:<roomId>' }.

parameters.name: pipeline
parameters.description: create-only: child ScheduledTask refs for onComplete/onSkip/onFail.

parameters.name: escalation
parameters.description: create-only: escalation ladder or explicit channel steps.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: create-only: structured task metadata.

parameters.name: idempotencyKey
parameters.description: create-only: stable key for deduping repeated schedules.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: create-only: low | medium | high (default medium).

parameters.name: respectsGlobalPause
parameters.description: create-only: when true, the task skips during global pause.

parameters.name: ownerVisible
parameters.description: create-only: when true, the task surfaces in owner views.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: create-only: task source (default_pack | user_chat | first_run | plugin).

parameters.name: minutes
parameters.description: snooze-only: minutes to defer next fire.

parameters.name: untilIso
parameters.description: snooze-only: ISO-8601 timestamp to defer next fire to.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: skip / complete / acknowledge / dismiss / reopen: free-form reason recorded on the state log.

parameters.name: patch
parameters.description: update-only: shallow patch of editable ScheduledTask fields.

parameters.name: sinceIso
parameters.description: history-only: ISO-8601 lower bound on log occurredAtIso.

parameters.name: untilHistoryIso
parameters.description: history-only: ISO-8601 upper bound on log occurredAtIso.

parameters.name: includeRollups
parameters.description: history-only: include rolled-up daily summary log rows (default false; raw rows only).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: history-only: row cap (default 100).

actions.descriptionCompressed: scheduled tasks: list|get|create|update|snooze|skip|complete|acknowledge|dismiss|cancel|reopen|history; kinds reminder|checkin|followup|approval|recap|watcher|output|custom

exampleCalls.user: Use SCHEDULED_TASKS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SHELL
actions.description: Execute a shell command via the configured local shell. Runs synchronously in the session cwd by default. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code. Hard timeout kills the command. Paths under the configured blocklist are off-limits as cwd.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command to run; executed via /bin/bash -c <command>.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Five to ten word humanly-readable summary of the command.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Hard timeout in ms; clamped to [100, 600000]. Default 120000.

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Absolute working directory; must not resolve under a blocked path. Defaults to the session cwd.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Run a shell command synchronously.

exampleCalls.user: Use SHELL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SHELL_HISTORY
actions.description: Shell command-history router. action=clear wipes the recorded history; action=view returns recent commands; action=disable is reserved for future use.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation: clear | view | disable. Inferred from message text when omitted (defaults to clear).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: For action=view: maximum number of history entries to return (default 20).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Shell history: clear | view | disable.

exampleCalls.user: Use SHELL_HISTORY with the provided parameters.

Action is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: subaction

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search term for action=search.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Search scope for action=search: all, products, orders, or customers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results per searched Shopify category.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Shopify: search, products, inventory, orders, customers.

exampleCalls.user: Use SHOPIFY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SKILL
actions.description: Manage skill catalog. Operations: search (browse available skills), details (info about a specific skill), sync (refresh catalog from registry), toggle (enable/disable installed skill), install (install from registry), uninstall (remove non-bundled skill). For invoking an enabled skill, use USE_SKILL instead.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Skill catalog: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall.

actions.name: TODO
actions.description: Manage the user's todo list. Actions: write (replace the list with `todos:[{id?, content, status, activeForm?}]`), create (add one), update (change by id), complete, cancel, delete, list, clear. Todos are user-scoped (entityId), persistent, and shared across rooms for the same user.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: write, create, update, complete, cancel, delete, list, clear.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Legacy alias for action.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Todo id (update/complete/cancel/delete).

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Imperative form, e.g. 'Add tests' (create/update).

parameters.name: activeForm
parameters.description: Present-continuous form, e.g. 'Adding tests' (create/update).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: pending | in_progress | completed | cancelled.

parameters.name: parentTodoId
parameters.description: Parent todo id for sub-tasks (create/update).

parameters.name: todos
parameters.description: Array of {id?, content, status, activeForm?} for action=write. Replaces the user's list for this conversation.

parameters.name: includeCompleted
parameters.description: Include completed/cancelled todos in action=list output.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return for action=list.

actions.descriptionCompressed: todos: write|create|update|complete|cancel|delete|list|clear; user-scoped (entityId)

exampleCalls.user: Use TODO with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TUNNEL
actions.description: Tunnel operations dispatched by `action`: start, stop, status. The `start` action accepts an optional `port` (defaults to 3000); `stop` and `status` take no parameters. Backed by whichever tunnel plugin is active (local Tailscale CLI, Eliza Cloud headscale, or ngrok).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Which tunnel sub-operation to run. One of: start, stop, status.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: Parameters forwarded to the selected sub-op. For `start`, optionally `{ port: number }`. `stop` and `status` take no parameters.

actions.name: UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES
actions.description: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end

parameters.name: timeZone
parameters.description: IANA time zone used to interpret preferred hours.

parameters.name: preferredStartLocal
parameters.description: Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

parameters.name: preferredEndLocal
parameters.description: Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

parameters.name: defaultDurationMinutes
parameters.description: Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).

parameters.name: travelBufferMinutes
parameters.description: Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).

parameters.name: blackoutWindows
parameters.description: Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.

actions.name: USE_SKILL
actions.description: Invoke an enabled skill by slug. The skill's instructions or script run and the result returns to the conversation.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Slug (canonical name) of an enabled skill to invoke. Must match a skill returned by the enabled_skills provider.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: How to invoke the skill: 'script' to run the bundled executable, 'guidance' to load the SKILL.md instructions, or 'auto' to pick automatically based on whether the skill ships scripts.

parameters.name: script
parameters.description: Optional script filename to run (used with mode='script' or mode='auto' when the skill has multiple scripts). Defaults to the first script in the skill.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Optional arguments to pass to the skill's script. Either an array of strings or a JSON object whose values become positional arguments.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Invoke an enabled skill by slug.

exampleCalls.user: Use USE_SKILL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: VOICE_CALL
actions.description: Owner-only. Place an outbound voice call via a registered provider. Action: `dial` with recipientKind=owner|external|e164. Current dispatch provider is Twilio; Android/app-phone is implementation-only until wired as a VOICE_CALL provider. Owner uses the env-configured owner number + standing escalation policy; external resolves a contact name via relationships then checks the allow-list; e164 dials a raw phone number. All paths draft first, require confirmed:true to dispatch, and use the approval queue.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Single canonical verb: `dial`.

parameters.name: recipientKind
parameters.description: Recipient discriminator: `owner` (escalation; uses owner number env), `external` (third party; recipient name resolved via relationships, allow-list checked), or `e164` (raw E.164 phone in `phoneNumber`).

parameters.name: phoneNumber
parameters.description: For recipientKind=e164: destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).

parameters.name: recipient
parameters.description: For recipientKind=external: contact name or E.164 phone number. Names resolve via the relationships store.

parameters.name: bodyText
parameters.description: Optional spoken message played when the call connects.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to actually place the call. Without it the action returns a draft / approval-queue entry.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional reason describing why the call is being placed (recorded with the approval task).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Twilio voice dial: recipientKind=owner|external|e164; draft-confirm; approval-queue

exampleCalls.user: Use VOICE_CALL with the provided parameters.
